hints: Word order: we first name the object (the food), then the adverb of degree (very much). The adverb of degree usually comes after the direct object.

a1.the food very much: ✅ «the food very much» — correct. The direct object (the food) always comes before the adverb of degree (very much). Example: *I didn't like the food very much last night.*
a1.very much the food: ❌ The adverb of degree (very much) does not come before the direct object (the food). Correct order: object → adverb.

hints: Word order: place (home) after the verb, then the manner/time adverb (very late). The place is closer to the verb than the time adverb.

a1.home very late: ✅ «home very late» — correct. We first indicate the place (home), then the time/manner adverb (very late). Example: *We arrived home very late.*
a1.very late home: ❌ The adverb (very late) does not come before the place (home). The place is closer to the verb.

hints: Word order: direct object (English) before the manner adverb (very well). The manner adverb comes after the object.

a1.English very well: ✅ «English very well» — correct. The direct object (English) comes before the manner adverb (very well). Example: *Teresa speaks English very well.*
a1.very well English: ❌ The manner adverb (very well) does not come before the object (English). Correct order: object → adverb.

hints: Word order: we first name the object (that box), then the place adverb (in the corner). We name the item first.

a1.that box in the corner: ✅ "that box in the corner" — correct. First we name the object (that box), then the place (in the corner). Example: *Can you put that box in the corner?*
a1.in the corner that box: ❌ The adverb of place (in the corner) does not precede the direct object (that box). The object is named first.

hints: Word order: the adverb of place (in hospital) precedes the adverb of time/duration (for a week). Place usually comes before time.

a1.in hospital for a week: ✅ "in hospital for a week" — correct. The adverb of place (in hospital) stands before the duration (for a week). Example: *Jim was in hospital for a week.*
a1.for a week in hospital: ❌ The duration of time (for a week) does not come before the place (in hospital). We first indicate the place.

hints: Word order: the object (this music) precedes the adverb of degree (a lot). The object is placed immediately after the verb.

a1.this music a lot: ✅ "this music a lot" — correct. The object (this music) stands before the adverb of degree (a lot). Example: *I like this music a lot.*
a1.a lot this music: ❌ The adverb of degree (a lot) does not come before the object (this music). The object is always after the verb.

hints: Word order: a more specific place (at a friend's house) precedes a broader context (after a party). From specific to general.

a1.at a friend's house after a party: ✅ "at a friend's house after a party" — correct. The specific place (at a friend's house) comes before the broader context (after a party). Example: *They met at a friend's house after a party.*
a1.after a party at a friend's house: ❌ The broader context (after a party) does not come before the specific place. Logic: from specific to general.

hints: Word order: the object (their victims) precedes the adverb of manner (very slowly). The object is closer to the verb.

a1.their victims very slowly: ✅ "their victims very slowly" — correct. The object (their victims) stands before the adverb of manner (very slowly). Example: *Tigers approach their victims very slowly.*
a1.very slowly their victims: ❌ The adverb of manner (very slowly) does not come before the object (their victims). The object is closer to the verb.

hints: Word order: first who/what (Hellen), then where (at the library). The object precedes the adverb of place.

a1.Hellen at the library: ✅ "Hellen at the library" — correct. First we name who was seen (Hellen), then where (at the library). Example: *I saw Hellen at the library.*
a1.at the library Hellen: ❌ The place (at the library) does not come before the object (Hellen). The person is named first.

hints: Word order: the object (the exam) precedes the adverb of manner (very quickly). The adverb of manner comes after the object.

a1.the exam very quickly: ✅ "the exam very quickly" — correct. The object (the exam) stands before the adverb of manner (very quickly). Example: *He finished the exam very quickly.*
a1.very quickly the exam: ❌ The adverb of manner (very quickly) does not come before the object (the exam). The object comes first.

hints: Word order: object (my dog) → place (in the park) → frequency (every day). Standard order of circumstances.

a1.my dog in the park every day: ✅ "my dog in the park every day" — correct. Order: object (my dog) → place (in the park) → frequency (every day). Example: *I walk my dog in the park every day.*
a1.my dog every day in the park: ❌ The frequency (every day) does not come before the place (in the park). Standard order: object → place → time.
a1.every day my dog in the park: ❌ The frequency (every day) does not start a phrase after the verb. The object must be immediately after the verb.

hints: Word order: object (her son) → direction (to the doctor) → time (this morning). The object is always after the verb.

a1.her son to the doctor this morning: ✅ «her son to the doctor this morning» — correct. Order: object (her son) → direction (to the doctor) → time (this morning). Example: *Lisa took her son to the doctor this morning.*
a1.to the doctor her son this morning: ❌ Direction (to the doctor) is not placed before the object (her son). The object is always first.
a1.this morning her son to the doctor: ❌ Time (this morning) does not start the phrase. Standard order: object → place → time.

hints: Word order: object (me) → place (home) → time (after the concert). Who to take → where → when.

a1.me home after the concert: ✅ «me home after the concert» — correct. Order: object (me) → place (home) → time (after the concert). Example: *Can you drive me home after the concert?*
a1.home me after the concert: ❌ Place (home) is not placed before the object (me). Who to take is first.
a1.me after the concert home: ❌ Time (after the concert) between the object and place disrupts the order. Where — before when.

hints: Word order: adverb of frequency (always) before the verb (finish), then object (school) → time (at 3.30 pm).

a1.always finish school at 3.30 pm: ✅ «always finish school at 3.30 pm» — correct. Adverb of frequency (always) before the verb, time at the end. Example: *We always finish school at 3.30 pm.*
a1.finish always school at 3.30 pm: ❌ Adverb of frequency (always) is not placed between the verb and the object. Frequency before the verb.
a1.finish school at 3.30 pm always: ❌ Adverb of frequency (always) usually does not stand at the end of such a sentence. Frequency before the verb.

hints: Word order: verb (like) → object (tennis) → adverb of degree (very much). Object before the adverb.

a1.don't like tennis very much: ✅ «don't like tennis very much» — correct. Verb → object → adverb of degree. Example: *I don't like tennis very much.*
a1.don't play very often tennis: ❌ This is another verb (play), not like. Moreover, the word order is incorrect.
a1.don't like very much tennis: ❌ Adverb of degree (very much) is not placed before the object (tennis). Object immediately after the verb.

hints: Word order: verb (loves) → object (his children) → adverb of degree (very much). Adverb of degree at the end.

a1.loves his children very much: ✅ «loves his children very much» — correct. Verb → object → adverb of degree. Example: *David loves his children very much.*
a1.very much loves his children: ❌ Adverb of degree (very much) is not placed before the verb. Adverb at the end.
a1.loves very much his children: ❌ Adverb of degree (very much) does not separate the verb and the object. Object immediately after the verb.

hints: Word order: object (Italian) → place (in Rome) → time/event (during a student exchange program). From main to details.

a1.Italian in Rome during a student exchang: ✅ «Italian in Rome during a student exchange program» — correct. Order: object (Italian) → place (in Rome) → time/event (during...). Example: *I learnt Italian in Rome during a student exchange program.*
a1.Italian during a student exchange progra: ❌ Event (during...) is not placed before the place (in Rome). Place is closer to the object.
a1.in Rome Italian during a student exchang: ❌ Place (in Rome) does not precede the object (Italian). The object is always after the verb.

hints: Word order: verb (had) → object (a nice lunch) → place (at a restaurant) → time (today). Standard sentence structure.

a1.had a nice lunch at a restaurant today: ✅ «had a nice lunch at a restaurant today» — correct. Order: verb → object → place → time. Example: *We had a nice lunch at a restaurant today.*
a1.had a nice lunch today at a restaurant: ❌ Time (today) cannot be placed before the location (at a restaurant). The location comes before the time.
a1.had at a restaurant a nice lunch today: ❌ Location (at a restaurant) does not separate the verb and the object. The object comes immediately after the verb.

hints: Word order: verb (forgot) → object (the keys) → location (in the car) → time (last night). The object comes before the location.

a1.forgot the keys in the car last night: ✅ "forgot the keys in the car last night" — correct. Order: verb → object → location → time. Example: *He forgot the keys in the car last night.*
a1.forgot in the car the keys last night: ❌ Location (in the car) does not precede the object (the keys). The object is mentioned first.
a1.forgot the keys last night in the car: ❌ Time (last night) cannot be placed before the location (in the car). The location comes before the time.

hints: Word order: adverb of frequency (often) before the verb (takes), then the object (a taxi) → direction (to work).

a1.often takes a taxi to work: ✅ "often takes a taxi to work" — correct. Adverb of frequency (often) before the verb, then object → direction. Example: *Susan often takes a taxi to work.*
a1.takes often a taxi to work: ❌ Adverb of frequency (often) cannot be placed between the verb and the object. Frequency comes before the verb.
a1.takes a taxi often to work: ❌ Adverb of frequency (often) does not separate the object and the direction. Frequency at the beginning.

hints: Word order: modal phrase (have to return) → object (the book) → location (to the library) → time (today).

a1.have to return the book to the library t: ✅ "have to return the book to the library today" — correct. Modal phrase → object → location → time. Example: *I have to return the book to the library today.*
a1.have to return today the book to the lib: ❌ Time (today) cannot be placed between the phrase and the object. Time at the end.
a1.have to return to the library the book t: ❌ Location (to the library) does not precede the object (the book). The object comes after the verb.

hints: Word order: verb be → adverb of frequency (never) → location (at home) → time (at lunchtime).

a1.are never at home at lunchtime: ✅ "are never at home at lunchtime" — correct. Verb be → adverb of frequency → location → time. Example: *They are never at home at lunchtime.*
a1.are never at lunchtime at home: ❌ Time (at lunchtime) cannot be placed before the location (at home). The location comes before the time.
a1.never are at home at lunchtime: ❌ Adverb of frequency (never) is placed after the verb be, not before it.

hints: Word order: verb (is) → adverb of frequency (always) → adjective (very hot) → location (here) → time (in July).

a1.is always very hot here in July: ✅ "is always very hot here in July" — correct. Be → adverb of frequency → adjective → location → time. Example: *It is always very hot here in July.*
a1.is very hot always here in July: ❌ Adverb of frequency (always) must stand immediately after the verb be, not in the middle.
a1.is always very hot in July here: ❌ Location (here) cannot be placed after the time (in July). The location comes before the time.

hints: Word order: verb (play) → object (football) → location (in the park) → time (after school).

a1.play football in the park after school: ✅ "play football in the park after school" — correct. Verb → object → location → time. Example: *We play football in the park after school.*
a1.play football after school in the park: ❌ Time (after school) cannot be placed before the location (in the park). The location comes before the time.
a1.play in the park football after school: ❌ The place (in the park) does not separate the verb and the object. The object is immediately after the verb.

hints: Word order: adverb of frequency (usually) before the verb (has), then the object (cereal) → circumstance (for breakfast).

a1.usually has cereal for breakfast: ✅ "usually has cereal for breakfast" — correct. The adverb of frequency before the verb, then the object → circumstance. Example: *She usually has cereal for breakfast.*
a1.has usually cereal for breakfast: ❌ The adverb of frequency (usually) is not placed between the verb and the object. Frequency before the verb.
a1.has cereal usually for breakfast: ❌ The adverb of frequency (usually) does not separate the object and the circumstance. Frequency at the beginning.

hints: Word order: verb (met) → object (her husband) → place (at a nightclub) → time (in 2006).

a1.met her husband at a nightclub in 2006: ✅ "met her husband at a nightclub in 2006" — correct. Verb → object → place → time. Example: *Linda met her husband at a nightclub in 2006.*
a1.met her husband in 2006 at a nightclub: ❌ Time (in 2006) is not placed before the place (at a nightclub). Place before time.
a1.met at a nightclub her husband in 2006: ❌ Place (at a nightclub) does not precede the object (her husband). Object after the verb.

hints: Word order: adverb of frequency (always) before the verb (work), then place (from home) → time (on Tuesdays).

a1.always work from home on Tuesdays: ✅ "always work from home on Tuesdays" — correct. The adverb of frequency before the verb, then place → time. Example: *I always work from home on Tuesdays.*
a1.work from home always on Tuesdays: ❌ The adverb of frequency (always) must be placed before the verb, not after the place.
a1.work always from home on Tuesdays: ❌ The adverb of frequency (always) is not placed between the verb and the place. Frequency before the verb.
